title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Install required packages for ContextStab: PyTorch Geometric for GNNs, fair-esm for protein language models, scanpy for scRNA-seq processing, and additional dependencies. Ensure all packages are compatible with CUDA for A6000 GPU.

steps.step1: Activate virtual environment: source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch Geometric and dependencies: pip install torch-geometric torch-scatter torch-sparse pyg-lib -f https://data.pyg.org/whl/torch-2.10.0+cu121.html
steps.step3: Install ESM-2: pip install fair-esm
steps.step4: Install additional bioinformatics tools: pip install biopython seaborn scikit-learn matplotlib
steps.step5: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step6: Create directory structure: mkdir -p data/{proteingym,megascale,hca,processed} models outputs figures

title: Download and Preprocess Protein Stability Datasets
description: Download ProteinGym benchmark datasets and Megascale stability data. These provide the ground truth ΔΔG values for training and evaluation. Use the official ProteinGym repository for consistent evaluation.

steps.step1: Clone ProteinGym repository: git clone https://github.com/OATML-Markslab/ProteinGym.git
steps.step2: Download ProteinGym substitutions benchmark: wget -P data/proteingym/ https://marks.hms.harvard.edu/proteingym/ProteinGym_substitutions.zip
steps.step3: Unzip and organize: unzip data/proteingym/ProteinGym_substitutions.zip -d data/proteingym/
steps.step4: Download Megascale dataset: Use wget to download from https://doi.org/10.1101/2022.12.06.519132 (or use ProteinGym's included Megascale variants)
steps.step5: Preprocess data: python scripts/preprocess_stability_data.py --input data/proteingym/ --output data/processed/stability_benchmarks.pkl
steps.step6: Create train/val/test splits: Use ProteinGym's predefined splits to ensure comparability with baselines
steps.step7: Verify data integrity: Check that we have ~2.5M variants across 283 proteins for training, S669 (669 variants) and Ssym (~600 variants) for testing

title: Download and Process REAL Human Cell Atlas scRNA-seq Data
description: Download real single-cell RNA-seq data from Human Cell Atlas (HCA) for 5 biologically distinct cell types. Use the Census API for programmatic access to harmonized scRNA-seq data. This addresses the critical feedback about using REAL data instead of synthetic contexts.

steps.step1: Install cellxgene-census: pip install cellxgene-census
steps.step2: Download real scRNA-seq for Plasma cells (bone marrow): python scripts/download_hca_data.py --cell_type 'plasma_cell' --tissue 'bone_marrow' --output data/hca/plasma_cells.h5ad
steps.step3: Download real scRNA-seq for Neurons (brain cortex): python scripts/download_hca_data.py --cell_type 'neuron' --tissue 'brain' --output data/hca/neurons.h5ad
steps.step4: Download real scRNA-seq for Hepatocytes (liver): python scripts/download_hca_data.py --cell_type 'hepatocyte' --tissue 'liver' --output data/hca/hepatocytes.h5ad
steps.step5: Download real scRNA-seq for Fibroblasts (lung): python scripts/download_hca_data.py --cell_type 'fibroblast' --tissue 'lung' --output data/hca/fibroblasts.h5ad
steps.step6: Download real scRNA-seq for Macrophages (multiple tissues): python scripts/download_hca_data.py --cell_type 'macrophage' --tissue 'lung,bone_marrow' --output data/hca/macrophages.h5ad
steps.step7: Extract proteostasis gene expression: python scripts/extract_proteostasis_genes.py --input_dir data/hca/ --output data/processed/cell_contexts.pkl --gene_list data/proteostasis_genes.txt
steps.step8: Validate cell type separation: Compute UMAP and verify distinct clustering by cell type

title: Build STRING Protein-Protein Interaction Network
description: Download and preprocess the STRING database to create the graph structure for the context encoder. Filter to high-confidence interactions and proteostasis-related genes.

steps.step1: Download STRING protein links (full network): wget -P data/string/ https://stringdb-static.org/download/protein.links.v12.0/9606.protein.links.v12.0.txt.gz
steps.step2: Download STRING protein info: wget -P data/string/ https://stringdb-static.org/download/protein.info.v12.0/9606.protein.info.v12.0.txt.gz
steps.step3: Gunzip files: gunzip data/string/*.gz
steps.step4: Filter for high-confidence interactions (combined_score > 700): python scripts/preprocess_string.py --input data/string/ --output data/processed/string_network.pkl --min_score 700
steps.step5: Extract proteostasis subgraph: python scripts/extract_proteostasis_subgraph.py --network data/processed/string_network.pkl --genes data/proteostasis_genes.txt --output data/processed/proteostasis_graph.pkl
steps.step6: Map genes to proteins: Create mapping from gene symbols to UniProt IDs using data/processed/gene_mapping.pkl

title: Baseline 1: ESM-2 Zero-Shot Stability Prediction
description: Implement and evaluate ESM-2 zero-shot predictions as the simplest baseline. This uses the pre-trained ESM-2 model without any task-specific training on stability data. Uses ESM-2 650M model.

steps.step1: Create baseline script: python baselines/esm2_zeroshot.py --model esm2_t33_650M_UR50D
steps.step2: Generate ESM-2 embeddings for all proteins in test sets
steps.step3: Compute mutation effect scores using ESM-2 log likelihood ratios: log P(mutant) - log P(wildtype)
steps.step4: Evaluate on ProteinGym test sets (S669, Ssym)
steps.step5: Compute metrics: Pearson correlation, Spearman correlation, RMSE between predicted and experimental ΔΔG
steps.step6: Save predictions to outputs/baselines/esm2_zeroshot_predictions.pkl
steps.step7: Expected performance: Pearson r ~0.40-0.50 based on literature (ESM-2 alone, no stability training)

title: Baseline 2: Fine-tuned ESM-2 on Stability Data
description: Fine-tune ESM-2 on Megascale/ProteinGym stability data. This represents a strong baseline that learns from the same training data as ContextStab but without context awareness.

steps.step1: Create fine-tuning script: python baselines/esm2_finetuned.py --model esm2_t33_650M_UR50D --train_data data/processed/stability_benchmarks.pkl
steps.step2: Freeze bottom 30 layers, fine-tune top 3 layers + prediction head
steps.step3: Training config: batch_size=8, learning_rate=1e-5, epochs=5, warmup_steps=1000
steps.step4: Train on Megascale (~260K variants) with 5-fold cross-validation
steps.step5: Run for 3 random seeds: 42, 123, 456
steps.step6: Evaluate on S669, Ssym test sets
steps.step8: Expected performance: Pearson r ~0.60-0.65 (competitive with Pro-PRIME on some benchmarks)

title: Baseline 3: PINNACLE + MLP for Stability Prediction
description: Use PINNACLE's context-aware protein embeddings as input to an MLP predictor for ΔΔG. This directly tests whether existing context-aware representations (without explicit stability training) can predict stability changes.

steps.step1: Download pre-trained PINNACLE embeddings from https://github.com/pnnl/pinnacle (or use API if available)
steps.step2: Create PINNACLE baseline script: python baselines/pinnacle_baseline.py
steps.step3: For each protein, extract PINNACLE embeddings for the cell types being evaluated
steps.step4: Train MLP head on top of PINNACLE embeddings: 2-layer MLP with hidden_dim=512
steps.step5: Training data: Megascale variants paired with cell-type-specific PINNACLE embeddings
steps.step6: Run for 3 random seeds: 42, 123, 456
steps.step7: Evaluate on test sets and save to outputs/baselines/pinnacle_mlp_seed{N}.pkl
steps.step8: Expected performance: Pearson r ~0.55-0.62 (PINNACLE provides context but lacks stability-specific training)

title: Baseline 4: Context-Agnostic GNN (Sequence + Graph Only)
description: Ablation baseline: ContextStab architecture without cellular context input. Tests whether the GNN architecture itself (without context information) provides any benefit over simple ESM-2 fine-tuning.

steps.step1: Create context-agnostic variant: python baselines/context_agnostic_gnn.py
steps.step2: Use same architecture as ContextStab but with zero/constant cell context vectors
steps.step3: This isolates the contribution of the real cellular context information
steps.step4: Train on Megascale with same hyperparameters as ContextStab
steps.step5: Run for 3 random seeds: 42, 123, 456
steps.step6: Evaluate and save to outputs/baselines/context_agnostic_seed{N}.pkl

title: Train ContextStab with REAL Human Cell Atlas Data
description: Train the full ContextStab model using real scRNA-seq data from Human Cell Atlas. This is the main proposed method integrating ESM-2 protein embeddings with real cellular contexts via Graph Attention Network.

steps.step1: Implement ContextStab architecture in models/contextstab.py
steps.step2: Architecture details: ESM-2 (650M, frozen) → 1280-dim embeddings
steps.step3: Context encoder: 3-layer GAT with hidden_dim=256, heads=4, dropout=0.1
steps.step4: Graph: Proteostasis gene network from STRING (high-confidence interactions)
steps.step5: Fusion: Cross-attention between protein embedding (query) and context graph (keys/values)
steps.step6: Prediction head: 2-layer MLP (512 → 256 → 1) predicting ΔΔG
steps.step7: Training: AdamW optimizer, lr=1e-4 with cosine decay, batch_size=16, epochs=10
steps.step8: Data pairing: Each protein mutation paired with all 5 real cell type contexts from HCA
steps.step9: Loss: MSE on ΔΔG + 0.01*contrastive_loss(encourage similar predictions for similar contexts)

title: Evaluate Context-Dependent Predictions Across Real Cell Types
description: Test the core hypothesis: Does ContextStab predict meaningful variation in stability across different real cell types from Human Cell Atlas? Evaluate predictions for the same mutations across plasma cells, neurons, hepatocytes, fibroblasts, and macrophages.

steps.step1: Load trained ContextStab model for each seed
steps.step2: For each mutation in test sets, predict ΔΔG for all 5 cell types
steps.step3: Compute prediction variance across cell types for each mutation
steps.step4: Identify mutations with cell-type-specific effects (>1 kcal/mol difference between max and min)
steps.step5: Test biological hypothesis: Plasma cells (high ER stress) show different predictions for secreted proteins vs. fibroblasts
steps.step6: Test biological hypothesis: Neurons show different predictions for proteins associated with neurodegeneration
steps.step7: Compute context sensitivity metric: Percentage of predictions varying by >1 kcal/mol across cell types
steps.step8: Success criterion: >20% of predictions show >1 kcal/mol variation across cell types

title: Ablation Study 1: Effect of Real vs Synthetic Contexts
description: Critical ablation to validate the importance of using REAL scRNA-seq data from Human Cell Atlas versus synthetic/artificial cellular contexts. This directly addresses the feedback about using real data.

steps.step1: Create synthetic context baseline: Generate 5 synthetic context vectors (random gene expression profiles)
steps.step2: Train ContextStab with synthetic contexts (same architecture, same training data)
steps.step3: Compare performance: Real HCA contexts vs Synthetic contexts
steps.step4: Metrics: Overall accuracy on S669/Ssym + context-sensitivity score
steps.step5: Expected: Real contexts should show higher context-sensitivity and potentially better overall accuracy if context information is useful
steps.step6: Run for 3 random seeds: 42, 123, 456

title: Ablation Study 2: Component Ablation - Context Encoder
description: Remove the Graph Attention Network context encoder and replace with simple MLP on gene expression. Tests whether the graph structure of protein interactions provides value.

steps.step1: Create ablation: Replace GAT with 2-layer MLP on flattened gene expression
steps.step2: Keep all other components identical (ESM-2, fusion, prediction head)
steps.step3: Train on same data with same hyperparameters
steps.step4: Run for 3 random seeds: 42, 123, 456
steps.step5: Compare: Full GAT vs MLP-only context encoding
steps.step6: Save results to outputs/ablations/no_graph_structure.pkl

title: Ablation Study 3: Component Ablation - Cross-Attention Fusion
description: Replace the cross-attention fusion mechanism with simple concatenation. Tests whether the learned attention-based fusion is necessary.

steps.step1: Create ablation: Replace cross-attention with concatenation + linear projection
steps.step2: Concatenate protein embedding and pooled context vector, project to 512-dim
steps.step3: Train on same data with same hyperparameters
steps.step4: Run for 3 random seeds: 42, 123, 456
steps.step5: Compare: Cross-attention fusion vs simple concatenation
steps.step6: Save results to outputs/ablations/no_cross_attention.pkl

title: Ablation Study 4: Gene Set Analysis
description: Test which gene sets contribute most to context-aware predictions. Compare full proteostasis gene set (~500 genes) vs chaperones only (~60 core chaperones) vs random gene sets.

steps.step1: Chaperone-only context: Use only 60 core chaperones from Brehme et al. (2014)
steps.step2: Full proteostasis: Chaperones + proteasome + autophagy + ER stress markers (~500 genes)
steps.step3: Random genes: Randomly select 500 genes as negative control
steps.step4: Train 3 variants with same architecture and hyperparameters
steps.step5: Run for 3 random seeds each: 42, 123, 456
steps.step6: Compare context-sensitivity across gene sets
steps.step7: Expected: Full proteostasis > Chaperones only > Random genes

resource_requirements: 1x A6000 (35GB VRAM), 3 random seeds x 3 variants

title: Zero-Shot Cell Type Transfer
description: Test whether ContextStab generalizes to unseen cell types from Human Cell Atlas. Train on 4 cell types, test on 1 held-out cell type, rotating through all combinations.

steps.step1: Create 5 train/test splits: Each leaving out one cell type
steps.step2: For each split: Train on 4 cell types, evaluate on held-out cell type
steps.step3: Metric: Prediction consistency (Spearman correlation between predictions on held-out vs training cell types)
steps.step4: Test if similar cell types (e.g., fibroblasts vs macrophages) transfer better than dissimilar ones
steps.step5: Run for 3 random seeds: 42, 123, 456
steps.step6: Save results to outputs/analysis/zero_shot_transfer.pkl

resource_requirements: 1x A6000 (45GB VRAM), 3 random seeds x 5 folds

title: Interpretability Analysis: Attention Patterns
description: Analyze the cross-attention weights to understand which cellular factors most influence predictions. Validate against known chaperone-client relationships from literature.

steps.step1: Extract attention weights from trained ContextStab model for all test predictions
steps.step2: Aggregate attention scores per gene across all predictions
steps.step3: Identify top-attended genes: Are they enriched for chaperones (HSP90, HSP70)?
steps.step4: Analyze by protein family: Do different proteins attend to different cellular factors?
steps.step5: Validate known relationships: Does HSP90 attention correlate with known HSP90 client proteins?
steps.step6: Compare attention patterns across cell types: Which genes distinguish plasma cells from neurons?
steps.step7: Create visualization: Heatmap of attention weights (proteins x genes)

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform rigorous statistical tests to validate that improvements are significant and not due to random chance. Use paired tests where appropriate.

steps.step1: For each metric (Pearson r, Spearman r, RMSE), compute mean and std across 3 seeds
steps.step2: Paired t-test between ContextStab and each baseline on matched predictions
steps.step3: Wilcoxon signed-rank test for non-parametric comparison
steps.step4: Effect size calculation: Cohen's d for magnitude of improvement
steps.step5: Multiple comparison correction: Bonferroni correction for testing against multiple baselines
steps.step6: Report 95% confidence intervals for all metrics
steps.step7: Save statistical test results to outputs/evaluation/statistical_tests.json

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Verify that the results meet all predefined success criteria from the proposal. Document which criteria are met, which are not, and why.

steps.step1: Criterion 1: Standard benchmark performance - Check if Pearson r > 0.65 on ProteinGym
steps.step2: Criterion 2: Context sensitivity - Check if >20% predictions vary by >1 kcal/mol across cell types
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Real data validation - Verify real HCA data outperforms synthetic contexts
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Interpretability - Check if attention correlates with known chaperone relationships
steps.step5: Document all results in outputs/evaluation/success_criteria.md
steps.step6: For any unmet criteria, analyze why and discuss limitations

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create publication-quality figures for the research paper. Include comparison plots, context-dependent prediction visualizations, and interpretability analysis.

steps.step1: Figure 1: Method overview diagram - ContextStab architecture with real HCA data flow
steps.step2: Figure 2: Benchmark comparison bar chart - ContextStab vs all baselines (ESM-2, Fine-tuned, PINNACLE) with error bars from 3 seeds
steps.step3: Figure 3: Context-dependent predictions heatmap - ΔΔG predictions for mutations (rows) across 5 cell types (columns)
steps.step4: Figure 4: Cell type variance distribution - Histogram of prediction variance across cell types
steps.step5: Figure 5: Ablation study results - Performance drop when removing each component
steps.step6: Figure 6: Attention interpretability - Top attended genes and their biological functions
steps.step7: Figure 7: Real vs Synthetic comparison - Side-by-side performance comparison
steps.step8: Save all figures to figures/ in PDF and PNG formats

title: Generate Results Tables
description: Create comprehensive results tables for the paper with all metrics, standard deviations, and statistical significance indicators.

steps.step1: Table 1: Main benchmark results - All methods on S669 and Ssym with Pearson/Spearman/RMSE
steps.step2: Table 2: Context sensitivity analysis - Variance statistics across cell types
steps.step3: Table 3: Ablation study results - Component contribution analysis
steps.step4: Table 4: Gene set comparison - Performance with different gene subsets
steps.step5: Table 5: Zero-shot transfer results - Generalization to held-out cell types
steps.step6: Save tables as LaTeX and CSV formats in figures/tables/
